name: Kartoffelklosse (Potato Dumplings)
recipeIngredient: 1 slice White bread, cut into 72 equal pieces
1 pound 14 ounces peeled cooked boiling potatoes, riced
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small skillet, heat the margarine until bubbly and hot.
2. Add the bread and sauté, stirring constantly, until the bread has absorbed the margarine and is browned. Set aside.
3. Reserve 2 tablespoons of flour in a measuring cup.
4. In a mixing bowl, combine the remaining flour with the potatoes, egg, and seasonings. Mix well to form a dough.
5. Portion the dough into 24 equal mounds.
6. Dust hands with the reserved flour and shape each mound into a ball.
7. Press three bread cubes into each ball and seal closed, forming dumplings.
8. In a 5-quart saucepan or Dutch oven, bring water to a boil.
9. Using a slotted spoon, gently lower several dumplings into the boiling water (they will sink to the bottom).
10. When the dumplings rise to the surface, cook for an additional 3 to 5 minutes.
11. With a slotted spoon, remove the dumplings to a warmed serving platter.
12. Repeat the process with the remaining dumplings.
These dumplings are good as an accompaniment to Sauerbarten.
Makes 12 servings of 2 dumplings each.
